Est. early 1990s

About Us

Three decades of folding, friendship, and the pursuit of an ancient art.

  • History
  • Mission
  • Philosophy
  • Organizing Team
Est. early 1990s

Where It All Started

The Greater San Diego Origami Group was founded in the early 1990s by two remarkable women whose passion for paper folding sparked a community that has flourished ever since.

Florence Temko was a prolific author who wrote dozens of books on origami and paper crafts, helping introduce the art to countless beginners. Her gift was making origami feel welcoming, joyful, and accessible to anyone willing to pick up a square of paper.

V'Ann Cornelius brought an artist's eye and a curator's vision. She co-curated Origami Masterworks, one of the first major museum-based origami exhibitions in the world, at Mingei International Museum in Balboa Park.

Together, they created a space where folders of all levels could gather, learn, and be inspired. That spirit endures today.

折

to fold

Why We Exist

Our Mission

To share the joy of paper folding, nurture its growth, bring people together, and encourage community among paper folders of all ages and experience levels. We welcome absolute beginners and experienced artists alike. What unites us isn't skill level — it's a shared wonder at what a single sheet of paper can become.

How We Learn Together

Our Philosophy

Origami enthusiasts of all ages and folding experiences are invited to participate in the mutual teaching of new and favorite models. Participants also share examples of their recent works — because teaching is itself a form of learning, and every folder has something to offer.

"Everyone is welcome. No experience necessary."
The People Behind the Group

Organizing Team

Perla Myers, PhD

Meeting Coordinator

Professor of Mathematics at the University of San Diego. Perla coordinates our folding meetings and brings origami into the mathematics classroom.
